title: Treat Accessibility as Barrier Removal
summary: Build and verify accessibility disability models in extreme-heat service barrier review through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain accessibility disability models from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply accessibility disability models without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

options.text: Extreme-heat service barrier review case 1: accessibility is a niche feature for a small fixed category of users.

Extreme-heat service barrier review case 1: build one separate accessible version after the standard product is complete.

steps.title: 04 · Read the extreme-heat service barrier review case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which claim preserves accessibility disability models when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: extreme-heat service barrier review.
steps.why: Treat Accessibility as Barrier Removal makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 4 in extreme-heat service barrier review.

content.text: Disability emerges from interaction between people, environments, tools, and design barriers. Visual, auditory, motor, speech, cognitive, vestibular, and neurological access needs may be permanent, temporary, situational, or overlapping. Barrier analysis starts with real tasks and conditions instead of guessing one fixed user category. Apply it to extreme-heat service barrier review.
